titleEn: Vacuum packaging gains ground in fresh produce supply chains
titleZh: 真空包装在生鲜供应链中应用渐广，但智慧畜牧关联有限
summaryZh: 本文介绍了真空包装技术在生鲜农产品供应链中的应用，重点阐述其通过减缓氧化和抑制微生物生长来延长保质期、减少采后损失，并提及在香草、蘑菇、干果、切菜及即食食品中的实际用途。文章还从可持续角度讨论了减少食物浪费的价值。然而，该技术主要聚焦于包装环节，与智慧畜牧核心关注的养殖业IoT、AI、自动化、机器人及传感器应用关联较弱，对种植业中采后处理的参考价值有限。
featuredReason: 虽然真空包装技术本身与智慧畜牧核心领域（养殖自动化、传感器、AI决策）关联度不高，但了解生鲜供应链中减少损耗的包装创新，对从事种养结合或冷链物流的智慧畜牧从业者有一定启发。
contentFull: Vacuum packaging gains ground in fresh produce supply chains Post-harvest losses continue to pressure the fresh produce industry, with moisture loss, oxidation, and physical damage during storage and transport accounting for a significant share of wasted product. As supply chains lengthen and retailers demand longer shelf life with less spoilage, packaging technology is playing an increasingly central role in how growers, processors, and distributors manage quality from farm to shelf. Temperature fluctuations, exposure to oxygen, and inconsistent handling between harvest and retail remain among the most common causes of quality degradation in fresh produce. For exporters in particular, extended transit times raise the stakes, since even minor packaging shortcomings can result in product arriving at its destination in unsellable condition. This has pushed many operations to reassess how they package goods at the point of processing, rather than relying solely on cold chain logistics. © Promaksvac Corp Vacuum packaging is one tool addressing these issues. By removing air from a package before sealing, vacuum sealing slows oxidation and limits the conditions that support microbial growth, which can extend usable shelf life for suitable products. The technology is not applicable to all fresh produce, but it has found practical use across categories including herbs, mushrooms, dried fruits, processed and cut vegetables, and prepared or ready-to-eat foods, where air exposure is a primary driver of quality loss. Commercial chamber vacuum sealers, used widely in food processing and packaging facilities, allow operators to apply consistent vacuum levels across batches, which supports more predictable shelf-life outcomes compared with manual or less controlled sealing methods. Beyond shelf-life extension, vacuum packaging is increasingly viewed through a sustainability lens. Reducing spoilage during storage and transit lowers food waste, while more stable shelf life gives processors and distributors greater flexibility in inventory planning and order fulfillment. For exporters managing longer transit windows, this can translate into fewer rejected shipments and reduced losses across the supply chain. © Promaksvac Corp Equipment manufacturers serving this space include Promarksvac Corporation, which produces commercial chamber vacuum sealers and vacuum packaging equipment for food processing, packaging, and industrial applications. Introvac is another supplier in the sector, offering commercial vacuum packaging and sealing equipment aimed at businesses seeking dependable, repeatable packaging performance across production runs. As fresh produce supply chains continue to navigate rising logistics costs and sustainability expectations, packaging equipment manufacturers are likely to keep refining vacuum sealing systems to handle a broader range of products and throughput demands. For an industry where product quality can be lost in the space between harvest and shelf, continued advances in food packaging equipment are expected to remain a focus area for processors and exporters alike.
